info.name: Hunter
info.description: The Hunter from Player’s Handbook 2024, Updated for Solandria
info.author: Wizards of the Coast

description.p: Protect Nature and People from Destruction
You stalk prey in the wilds and elsewhere, using your abilities as a Hunter to protect nature and people everywhere from forces that would destroy them.
You can call on the forces of nature to reveal certain strengths and weaknesses of your prey. You always have the Hunter's Mark spell prepared as one of your Favored Enemy spells.

While a creature is marked by your Hunter's Mark, you know whether that creature has any Immunities, Resistances, or Vulnerabilities, and if the creature has any, you know what they are. After gaining this knowledge, you ignore any applicable damage Resistances to any damage you inflict with your attacks or spells against that creature.

sheet.description: You always have the Hunter's Mark spell prepared as one of your Favored Enemy spells; it does not count against the total number of spells you can prepare with that feature. While a creature is marked by your Hunter's Mark, you know its Immunities, Resistances, or Vulnerabilities, and your spells and attacks ignore any damage Resistances it has against them.

description.p: Colossus Slayer. Your tenacity can wear down even the most resilient foes. When you hit a creature with a weapon, the weapon deals an extra 1d8 damage to the target if it's missing any of its Hit Points. You can deal this extra damage only once per turn.

Horde Breaker. Once on each of your turns when you make an attack with a weapon, you can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target, that is within the weapon's range, and that you haven't attacked this turn.

Your tenacity can wear down even the most resilient foes. When you hit a creature with a weapon, the weapon deals an extra 1d8 damage to the target if it's missing any of its Hit Points. You can deal this extra damage only once per turn.

sheet.description: When you hit a creature that's missing any of its Hit Points with a weapon, you deal an extra 1d8 damage to it.

description.p: Once on each of your turns when you make an attack with a weapon, you can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target, that is within the weapon's range, and that you haven't attacked this turn.

sheet.description: When you make an attack with a weapon, you can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5ft of the original target, that is within the weapon's range, and that you haven't attacked this turn.

description.p: Escape the Horde. When you take the Attack action, you may also take the Disengage action as part of that same action.
Multiattack Defense. When a creature hits you with an attack roll, that creature has Disadvantage on all other attack rolls against you this turn.
Focused Mind. When you fail a Saving Throw triggered by a creature marked by your Hunter's Mark spell, you can use your Reaction to reroll the saving throw.

select.name: Escape the Horde
select.supports: THC Defensive Tactics Escape the Horde

element.name: Escape the Horde
element.supports: THC Defensive Tactics Escape the Horde

description.p: When you take the Attack action, you may also take the Disengage action as part of that same action.

sheet.description: When you take the Attack action, you may also take the Disengage action as part of that same action.

description.p: When a creature hits you with an attack roll, that creature has Disadvantage on all other attack rolls against you this turn.

sheet.description: When a creature hits you with an attack roll, that creature has Disadvantage on all other attack rolls against you this turn.

description.p: When you fail a Saving Throw triggered by a creature marked by your Hunter's Mark spell, you can use your Reaction to reroll the saving throw.

sheet.description: When you fail a Saving Throw triggered by a creature marked by your Hunter's Mark, you can reroll the saving throw.

description.p: Once per turn when you deal damage to a creature marked by your Hunter's Mark, the target takes two rolls of the spell's damage die instead of just one.

sheet.description: When you deal damage to a creature marked by your Hunter's Mark, the target takes two rolls of the spell's damage die instead of just one.
